about_text: This app helps you learn unfamiliar alphabets. The idea is to read text in your own language, gradually replacing characters with letters from another alphabet. This way, you memorize them in a natural context. Perfect for reading fairy tales to children and learning a new script at the same time!
sample_placeholder: For example: Arthur and Anna went to school...
transcription_subtitle: Latin SMS text → native alphabet for translation apps
transcription_hint: Paste text typed in Latin the way people write that language in chat (romanization). Tap Convert to get native script, then copy into any translator.
tips_text:
1. Start with vowels. First, replace only the vowels with letters from the new alphabet. This is easier and helps you get used to the new symbols.

2. Don't rush! Don't try to replace all the letters at once. Read familiar text, gradually adding new letters so your brain can adapt.

3. Read with partial replacements. Read the same familiar text for a long time, gradually increasing the number of replaced letters. This is more effective than switching to full replacement immediately.

4. Move to consonants after vowels. Once you've memorized the vowels, start replacing consonants.

5. Leave complex paired sounds for later. Combinations like CH, TCH, DJ are best learned after you've mastered individual vowels and consonants.

6. Use pop-up options for similar sounds. If a letter has several writing options, choose the most suitable one via the pop-up.
feedback_email_invalid: That does not look like a valid email
feedback_success: Thank you — your message was sent.
feedback_error: Could not send. Please try again later.
